plan.title: Port triage:queue (ranking + RESUME/ORPHAN/URGENT order) to TypeScript

narratives.Description: Port scripts/triage_queue.py to a TypeScript triage queue module, preserving the candidate ranking order and the [RESUME], [ORPHAN], and [URGENT] classification tags exactly. This is the highest-risk triage slice because the ranking order is observable in operator output, so it ships a golden parity binary; it is independently buildable in its own packages/core/src/triage/queue subdirectory.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Create the packages/core/src/triage/queue module that ports the candidate ranking comparator and the RESUME/ORPHAN/URGENT tagging from triage_queue.py, with vitest unit tests in the module directory.
2. Add a packages/cli/src/triage-queue-parity.ts parity binary that runs the TS queue and the Python scripts/triage_queue.py oracle and asserts byte-identical ranked output with cache off.
3. Verify the port with the new vitest tests and the parity binary while the Python module remains the authoritative oracle.
narratives.UserStory: As an operator asking what to work on next, I want triage:queue available in TypeScript, so that the ranked work queue and its RESUME/ORPHAN/URGENT tags come from the TS engine with the same order as the Python module.

narrative.Acceptance: Running triage:queue on the TS port returns candidates in the same ranked order the Python module produces for an identical cache.
The TS queue renders the [RESUME], [ORPHAN], and [URGENT] tags on the same candidates the Python module tags.
Running the triage-queue parity binary returns exit 0 and renders byte-identical output to the Python triage_queue.py oracle with cache off.

expected_outputs: New packages/core/src/triage/queue module with vitest unit tests
Byte-identical parity vs scripts/triage_queue.py under cache-off
swarm.missing_traces_justification: #1530 Wave-3 framework-internal TS port traced to GitHub issue #1725 and umbrella epic #1530; no specification.vbrief.json spec-section applies to maintainer tooling.

references.title: Issue #1725: feat(ts-migration): port the triage:* family to TS (Wave 3)
